fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Patio Area; Pedestrian Walkways; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the accessibility and navigability of the property for emergency responders could be beneficial. Improved marking and signage of pedestrian walkways and access points may assist in navigating multiple structures rapidly during an emergency situation. Consideration could be given to providing more unobstructed access routes, especially to the rear areas, to facilitate easier and quicker deployment of emergency equipment. Strategically maintained open green spaces could serve as effective staging areas, providing clear separation for different operational purposes. Properly simplified navigation around the patio and landscaped areas may enhance responder movement and visibility, supporting overall emergency operations.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Tree Cluster; Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Fence; Open Fuel Break
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by managing vegetation adjacent to structures and maintaining separation from scattered trees may reduce fuel continuity and decrease potential fire exposure. Managing the density and distribution of vegetation along the perimeter and adjacent to fences could improve the ability to limit fire spread toward structures. Addressing the tree cluster through careful thinning or spacing could further enhance fuel separation and reduce the opportunity for crown fire development. Preservation of existing open fuel breaks should be prioritized as they play a crucial role in hindering fire progression across the landscape. Regular upkeep of these areas will support firefighter access and suppression efforts. These actions, combined with ongoing maintenance of surrounding vegetation, may enhance long-term wildfire resilience and improve overall property preparedness.
